data.title: Over 3,000 Britons file lawsuit against Johnson & Johnson for alleged cancer-causing baby powder.
data.description: Over 3,000 individuals in the UK have become part of a legal case claiming that the corporation was aware that its baby powder contained cancer-causing fibers, such as asbestos.

data.title: Commercial Metals Co. beats Q4 expectations with $2.115B net sales, $1.37 EPS (63% YoY increase), $1.35 diluted earnings/share. Strong EBITDA of $291.4M, cash equivalents at $1B+. CEO optimistic about transformative strategy.
data.description: Commercial Metals Company reported fourth-quarter net sales of $2.115 billion, exceeding analysts' expectations of $2.084 billion, representing a 6% increase from the previous year. The company's adjusted earnings per share were $1.37, slightly above the forecast of $1.36 per share, indicating a 63% increase from the previous year. The company's net after-tax earnings of $1.35 per diluted share included charges related to interest expenses, impairment charges, and unrealized gains on commodity hedges. Commercial Metals Company reported a core EBITDA of $291.4 million with a margin of 13.8%, showing improvement both sequentially and year over year. The company maintained a strong balance sheet with $1.0 billion in cash and cash equivalents and total available liquidity of approximately $1.9 billion as of August 31, 2025. CEO Peter Matt highlighted the company's transformative strategy for the future.

data.title: Morrow Hill partners with Extraordinary Brands to boost growth. Dallas-based real estate firm to support boutique fitness and wellness expansion.
data.description: Two major players in the industry have joined forces to drive national expansion in boutique fitness and wellness brands. The collaboration between Morrow Hill, a leading commercial real estate firm in Dallas recognized for franchise tenant representation, was revealed on October 16, 2025. This partnership aims to boost growth across the sector, leveraging the strengths of both companies to maximize market penetration. This announcement marks a significant step towards expanding their reach and influence in the fitness and wellness market segment.

data.title: Wireless Zone appoints Al Pellecchia as EVP, improving leadership team of largest Verizon franchisor.
data.description: The leading Verizon franchise operator in the country has strengthened its executive team by bringing on a seasoned industry executive. In a move to enhance sales and empower their system further, Wireless Zone® based in Fishers, Indiana, announced on October 16, 2025, that a longtime franchisee will assume a leadership position within the company.

data.title: Facteus generates 76% alpha with exclusive transaction data, leading in consumer intelligence for investors.
data.description: Maiden Century's recent study reveals that Facteus provides the highest, quickest, and most robust alpha for investors. Facteus, a top player in real-time consumer intelligence, shared that its consumer transaction data offers unmatched performance. This information was disclosed on October 16, 2025, in Portland, Oregon.

data.title: Rebecca Pirillo appointed as COO of ERPHealth, leading in behavioral health solutions. Oct 16, 2025.
data.description: In Philadelphia on October 16, 2025, ERPHealth, a top provider of solutions for tracking behavioral health outcomes and managing population health, revealed the selection of Rebecca Pirillo as the new Chief Operating Officer. In her new position, Pirillo will be responsible for managing operations and strategy within the company.

data.title: Backroads announces 11 new itineraries for 2026 with Casual Hotels in global destinations to cater to active travelers.
data.description: Today in BERKELEY, Calif., Backroads, a key player in active travel, revealed 11 fresh itineraries for 2026 highlighting Casual Hotels from Patagonia to Japan. These journeys offer a blend of comfortable inns, lodges and boutique hotels, tailored for active travelers seeking unique experiences. The initiative aims to cater to the growing demand for travel options that combine adventure with cultural immersion. With this announcement, Backroads reinforces its status as a top choice for travelers seeking authentic and active vacations, expanding its portfolio to over 50 countries worldwide.

data.title: France's Lightning Stock Exchange plans the first tokenized IPO in Q1 2026, aiming to revolutionize equity trading in Europe.
data.description: France's Lightning Stock Exchange is set to launch the first tokenized IPO in the first quarter of 2026. The exchange aims to establish itself as Europe's leading fully tokenized equity exchange.

data.title: Travelers Companies reports strong Q3 earnings with $12.47 billion in sales, $8.14 EPS, and core income of $1.87 billion, driven by lower catastrophe losses.
data.description: Shares of Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) dropped in premarket trading following the release of their third-quarter results for FY25. The company reported a 5% increase in sales, reaching $12.47 billion, surpassing analyst expectations of $11.81 billion. Their adjusted earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $8.14, beating the estimated $6.29. Core income saw a significant rise to $1.87 billion from $1.22 billion in the previous year's quarter, driven by reduced catastrophe losses, a higher underlying underwriting gain, and an uptick in net investment income. Additionally, net realized investment gains were $27 million pre-tax ($21 million after-tax), marking a decrease from the previous year's $55 million pre-tax. To read the full article, visit Benzinga.com.
